title: Cut VS Code extension to two surfaces: read-only Why + write-only Capture
context: Extension ballooned to 20 commands, 4 webviews, tree view, codelens, diagnostics, decorations—unmaintainable scope the author could no longer explain. Core value (decision memory) drowned in noise.
decision: Delete 9 files and 2,176 lines. Keep only: (1) 'Hunch: Why is this?' surface (file brief, symbol context, invariants, bug history), (2) 'Hunch: Capture…' picker for writing decisions. All mutations delegated to hunch CLI or MCP write path; extension never edits .hunch/ JSON directly. Remove: Change Gate, all command hub, diagnostics, decorations, webviews.
consequences: Single, defensible value proposition: see why + capture what you decided
All memory mutations auditable (goes through MCP/CLI, not direct edits)
Maintenance surface shrinks enough to stay coherent
alternatives_rejected: Hide unused features instead of deleting (rejected: false flag burden; deletion is cleaner)
Keep Change Gate + other capture UIs (rejected: duplicate picker surfaces)
Maintain backward compatibility (rejected: breaking API acceptable for clarity)

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Hide unused features instead of deleting (rejected: false flag burden; deletion is cleaner)
Keep Change Gate + other capture UIs (rejected: duplicate picker surfaces)
Maintain backward compatibility (rejected: breaking API acceptable for clarity)
